reforms.description: Estonia aims to further encourage low-carbon technology innovation by expanding public R&D investment and increasing the share of funding directed towards environment-related issues.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D investment in low-carbon technologies is expected to stimulate innovation, enhance productivity, and support the transition to a sustainable economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy to expand public R&D investment was announced as part of the efforts to transition to a low-carbon economy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding priorities towards environmental issues, which is crucial for addressing climate change and fostering innovation in low-carbon technologies.
reforms.source_quote: Further encourage low-carbon technology innovation by expanding public R&D investment and by increasing the share of funding on environment-related issues.

reforms.description: Since 2020, Enterprise Estonia has been offering both consulting and financial support for conducting applied research, aimed at enhancing innovation capabilities among businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to enhance the innovation capacity of firms, leading to increased productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: Enterprise Estonia has been actively providing support since 2020.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant enhancement of existing support mechanisms for applied research, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: For innovation, since 2020 Enterprise Estonia offers both consulting and financial support for conducting applied research.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Education and Research has initiated sectoral research programmes tailored to the needs of entrepreneurs, aiming to align research efforts with market demands.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning research with the specific needs of industries, this initiative is expected to enhance the relevance and application of research outcomes, driving economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry has initiated these programmes based on the needs of entrepreneurs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly addresses the gap between research and market needs, fostering innovation in key sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Education and Research has also initiated sectoral research programmes based on the needs of entrepreneurs.

reforms.description: Estonia is implementing a programme to improve managerial practices and organizational performance of firms, which includes a strong element of network-building to disseminate good practices and mutual learning.
reforms.package_name: Estonian Research and Development, Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Plan 2021-2035
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing managerial practices can lead to improved organizational performance, which in turn supports innovation and productivity in firms.
reforms.status_evidence: Programmes and activities targeted to disseminate good practice and mutual learning will be continued and developed.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the capabilities of firms through improved management, which is crucial for fostering innovation and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Improvement of managerial practices and organizational performance of firms is one of the action targeted throughout Estonian Research and Development, Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Plan 2021-2035.

reforms.description: Estonia plans to expedite the awards of 5G bands to meet its ambitious goal of deploying 5G in major cities by 2023 and along transport corridors by 2025, enhancing digital infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital infrastructure is essential for enhancing connectivity, which can drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Estonia plans to expedite the awards of 5G bands.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant step towards enhancing digital infrastructure, which is crucial for future economic development and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Estonia plans to expedite the awards of 5G bands to meet the country’s ambitious goal to deploy 5G in major cities by 2023.

reforms.description: The e-CMR experiment conducted by Estonia and its Baltic neighbors aims to improve logistics efficiency through digital solutions, facilitating cross-border exchange of electronic freight information.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining logistics processes through digital solutions, the initiative enhances efficiency and reduces costs, benefiting the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The tests were successful and the idea of the e-CMR indexing scheme proved its benefits to all parties in the supply chain.
reforms.importance_rationale: The e-CMR experiment represents a significant innovation in logistics, with potential long-term benefits for cross-border trade and efficiency.
reforms.source_quote: The countries used real-time freight information to reduce paperwork in the road transport sector and unjustified downtime during the roadside checks.

reforms.description: Estonia plans to boost R&D to support the development of necessary future decarbonisation technologies to reach net zero emissions by 2050.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D in decarbonisation technologies is expected to drive innovation and productivity in the energy sector, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Plans to boost R&D for decarbonisation technologies have been outlined.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant commitment to innovation in the energy sector, which is crucial for achieving environmental goals and enhancing economic resilience.
reforms.source_quote: A boost to R&D will be required to support the development of necessary future decarbonisation technologies to reach net zero emissions by 2050.

reforms.description: Estonia is considering the development of small modular reactors (SMRs) to enhance its energy security and support climate neutrality goals, with plans for the first reactor to be operational by the late 2020s.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The development of SMRs is expected to provide stable, zero-carbon electricity, contributing to the transition to a low-carbon economy and enhancing energy independence.
reforms.status_evidence: A working group on nuclear energy has been formed to analyze the possibility of nuclear energy in Estonia.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant shift towards nuclear energy, which could play a crucial role in Estonia's energy mix and climate goals, marking a major policy direction.
reforms.source_quote: Estonia is considering the use of nuclear energy... A working group on nuclear energy... has been formed in 2020 to analyse the possibility of nuclear energy in Estonia.

reforms.description: Estonia has merged its previous research and development strategies into a new joint development plan for research, innovation, and entrepreneurship for 2021-2035, aiming to enhance its innovation capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The new strategy aims to increase public R&D funding, which is essential for fostering innovation and addressing environmental challenges.
reforms.status_evidence: The elements have been merged into a new joint development plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in Estonia's approach to R&D and innovation, aiming to align efforts with environmental goals and enhance overall innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: In 2021, these elements have been merged into a new joint development plan for research and development, innovation and entrepreneurship for 2021-2035.

reforms.description: Estonia aims to increase overall public R&D funding and shift its focus towards environment-related issues to support the transition to a low-carbon economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding will enhance innovation in low-carbon technologies, contributing to environmental sustainability and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Estonia should further develop its innovation capacity by increasing overall public R&D funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses the need for increased funding in a specific area, but it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: To support the transition to a low-carbon economy, Estonia should further develop its innovation capacity by increasing overall public R&D funding.

reforms.description: Estonia has provided subsidies to establish biomethane filling stations and awarded grants to public bus operators that use biomethane as a fuel, promoting the use of cleaner energy in public transport.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative supports the transition to cleaner fuels in public transport, which can reduce emissions and improve public health, contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Estonia has been providing subsidies and grants since 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant investment in cleaner public transport, which has implications for environmental sustainability and public health.
reforms.source_quote: Estonia has provided subsidies to establish biomethane filling stations and has awarded grants to public bus operators that use it as a fuel.

reforms.description: The new building code introduced in 2013 requires all new public sector buildings to comply with nearly zero-energy building standards, significantly improving energy efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving energy efficiency in public buildings, the initiative reduces energy costs and promotes sustainable practices, which can enhance economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: From 2019, all new public sector buildings must comply with the new standard.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in building standards that will have long-term impacts on energy consumption and sustainability in the public sector.
reforms.source_quote: From 2019, all new public sector buildings must comply with the new standard while all new private buildings must do so from 2021 onwards.
